name: Authentication bypass via improper serialization
description: Improper serialization of internal authentication data in MongoDB Server allows an authenticated user to bypass authorization restrictions.

name: Shell script injection via mongosh
description: MongoDB Shell (mongosh) is vulnerable to OS command injection when processing certain query results containing specially crafted field names.

name: BSON buffer overread
description: An incorrect bounds check in the BSON parser allows reading past the end of the buffer, potentially exposing sensitive memory content.

name: Integer overflow in memory allocation
description: An integer overflow when processing a specifically crafted aggregation request causes an out-of-bounds memory access, leading to server crash.

name: TLS peer certificate validation bypass
description: MongoDB Server allows connections that should require TLS peer certificate validation to succeed without proper verification.

name: Text search crash via invalid regex
description: A malformed query with $text and $regex operators can crash the MongoDB server due to improper memory handling.

name: World-readable log file
description: MongoDB log files are created with world-readable permissions, potentially exposing sensitive operational data.
recommendation: Set restrictive file permissions on MongoDB log files.

name: Session reuse after logout
description: After a user has been deleted, their session remains valid until expired, allowing continued access to the database.

name: LDAP authentication bypass
description: Improper handling of LDAP authentication responses allows users to authenticate with incorrect credentials under certain configurations.

name: Query planner denial of service
description: A specially crafted query can cause the query planner to consume excessive memory and CPU, leading to denial of service.

name: Field name injection in Go driver
description: The MongoDB Go Driver allows injection of field names in BSON documents when using certain marshal functions with untrusted input.

name: Aggregation operator abuse DoS
description: Certain aggregation pipeline operators can be abused to consume excessive resources, causing denial of service.

name: SCRAM-SHA1 authentication bypass
description: A flaw in SCRAM-SHA1 authentication allows an attacker to bypass authentication under specific race conditions.

name: nativeHelper buffer overflow RCE
description: Buffer overflow in the nativeHelper function of MongoDB's SpiderMonkey JS engine allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

name: Cursor invalidation DoS
description: A specially crafted query that forces cursor invalidation during certain operations can cause the MongoDB server to crash.
